frameworks.name: structure learning
frameworks.one_line: stereotypes governed by probabilistic beliefs about assignment of individuals to groups

frameworks.name: Bayesian latent cause inference
frameworks.one_line: flexible, hierarchical group construction over latent group assignments

frameworks.name: hierarchical clustering / nested mixture
frameworks.one_line: nested groups enable subgrouping as refinement of a parent group

core_question: Why, when, and how do stereotypes change as a function of structure learning over group assignments?
key_assumptions: stereotypes are probabilistic beliefs about assignment of individuals to groups
groups are flexibly constructed from the distribution of traits across individuals
structure learning principles govern belief updating about group identity
key_findings: the model explains subtyping by segregating deviant individuals from a group to protect its stereotype
the model explains subgrouping by organising deviant individuals into a refinement (nested subgroup)
perceived variability, typicality, cognitive load, and sample size modulate stereotype change
interpretations: stereotype change is naturally accounted for by structure learning over flexible, hierarchically organised group representations
